llm_operation.reason: Both PyTorch and PaddlePaddle NLLLoss are class-based modules requiring explicit forward calls with 'input' (log-probabilities) and 'target'/'label' (class indices). The original test cases only instantiate the loss module without calling forward or providing required positional arguments. According to docs, NLLLoss expects two inputs: input tensor (log-probabilities of shape [N, C]) and target tensor (class indices of shape [N] with values in [0, C-1]). The repair adds minimal valid forward call inputs: target/label of shape [2] (matching batch size), dtype int64 for PyTorch (required for target) and int64 for Paddle (consistent with label requirements), and ensures proper instantiation + forward invocation semantics.
